articles.author: Washington Post staff
articles.content:
Incumbent Sen. Bill Cassidy runs in the Republican primary that decides who will face the Democrat nominee in November.

articles.description: Live updates and results in the Louisiana Senate primary elections for Democrats and Republicans as incumbent Bill Cassidy runs.
articles.title: Live updates: Louisiana Senate primary election results 2026

articles.author: Teo Armus, Gregory S. Schneider
articles.content: Even after a Supreme Court ruling on Friday put a final stop to Democratic efforts to redraw Virginias congressional maps, the state will remain central to the partys battle to win control of the Hou… [+14 chars]
articles.description: Republicans won the redistricting war in Virginia, but several lawmakers remain vulnerable.
articles.title: Virginia’s new House map is dead. Democrats think they can still flip these seats.

articles.author: Victoria Craw, Rachel Chason, Dan Lamothe
articles.content: U.S. and Nigerian forces carried out a joint operation that killed a senior Islamic State commander in Nigeria, U.S. and Nigerian military authorities said, in a move they called a strategic blow to … [+18 chars]
articles.description: Trump said the operation killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, whom he described as « second in command of ISIS globally. » Nigeria's president said the strike took place at a compound in the Lake Chad basin.
articles.title: Trump says top Islamic State leader was killed in Nigeria strike

articles.author: Theodoric Meyer
articles.content: Sen. Bill Cassidy (Louisiana) was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Donald Trump in his impeachment trial for his role in fomenting the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the Capit… [+2 chars]
articles.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana, one of seven Republicans who voted to convict Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial, is in danger of losing his primary.
articles.title: GOP senator who defied Trump on impeachment faces voters, five years later

articles.content:

articles.author: Danielle Zickl
articles.content:
Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is one of the most common hormonal disorders out there, affecting about 170 million women worldwide and roughly 1 in 10 women of reproductive age in the U.S.
Now, it… [+141 chars]
articles.description: The PCOS name change to PMOS aims to reflect the full scope of the condition — and finally unlock the care women have been seeking for years.
articles.title: Women with this condition are often misdiagnosed. Its new name could help.

articles.author: Mariana Alfaro
articles.content: ASHEVILLE, N.C. Fifth-generation farmer Jamie Ager won the Democratic nomination in a swingy, rural North Carolina House district in March. Now, he faces two opponents: a well-known Republican and hi… [+18 chars]
articles.description: In North Carolina, Jamie Ager is distancing himself from his national party to win a congressional seat.
articles.title: A North Carolina farmer is testing whether Democrats can still win rural America

articles.description: Eating the right nutrients can help you in the fight to maintain bone mass as you age. These foods are high in what you need.
articles.title: 5 foods to support strong bones

articles.author: Maria Sacchetti
articles.content:
After U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement detained his mother, 2-year-old Orlin Hernandez Reyes moved into a shed.
His uncle, Samuel Maldonado Erazo, was charged with taking care of the toddler… [+135 chars]
articles.description: ICE accused Wendy Hernandez Reyes of leaving her child with a violent uncle, but she says her son would still be alive if officers hadn’t detained and deported her.
articles.title: She was deported without her toddler. Then ICE blamed her for his killing.

articles.author: Steve Thompson
articles.content: In the last conversation Brady Dale Flowers II had with his mother, she told him to be careful about the friends he kept. A few weeks earlier, hed thrown a rooftop cookout without permission, some 30… [+108 chars]

articles.title: His mother warned him to be careful. By evening, the D.C. student was dying.
